claims.text: Token budget tracking uses a `BudgetTracker` struct with: continuationCount, lastDeltaTokens, lastGlobalTurnTokens, startedAt. This is a pure value object, not a global; it is created fresh per query loop via createBudgetTracker().

When a token budget is configured and the agent is under 90% of the budget, the engine sends a 'continue' decision with a nudge message injected as a meta user message ('Output token limit hit. Resume directly - no apology...') to push the model to continue.
Diminishing returns detection: if continuationCount >= 3 AND the last two delta token increments are both < 500, the engine stops continuing regardless of budget percentage.

Context compaction has four strategies: (1) autocompact (proactive threshold-based), (2) reactive compact (triggered on prompt-too-long API error), (3) snip (selective pruning of non-essential messages), (4) context-collapse (staged collapse of read/search messages). These are layered, not mutually exclusive.
autocompact runs before every API call; its result (if fired) replaces messagesForQuery with postCompactMessages. The tracking struct resets on each compact so per-compact turn counts are accurate.
After a compact boundary, messages before the boundary are released for GC. The boundary message index is spliced out so only post-boundary messages are kept in mutableMessages.

Tool result size is bounded by a per-tool `maxResultSizeChars` field. Tools with large results (default) get their output persisted to disk and replaced with a file-path pointer. Tools where circular reads would result (e.g. FileReadTool) set maxResultSizeChars to Infinity to disable disk offload.
